task_completion.description: Locate the renal cyst in the right kidney on the abdominal CT.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Medical imaging software (e.g., 3D Slicer) is open, displaying an abdominal CT scan with the right kidney in view, and initial markups or crosshairs are placed on a cyst.

task_completion.description: Measure the maximum diameter of the cyst.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A ruler or length measurement markup is visible across the cyst in the imaging software, displaying the diameter in millimeters.

task_completion.description: Measure the internal HU density of the cyst and the reference parenchyma HU.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Region of Interest (ROI) or probe tools are placed both inside the cyst and on the surrounding normal kidney tissue, displaying Hounsfield Unit (HU) statistics (mean/range).

task_completion.description: Save the measurement markups and the structural Bosniak report JSON files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal, file explorer, or text editor shows that `cyst_measurements.mrk.json` and `bosniak_report.json` have been created in `~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/`. The report contains the required fields (cyst_location, max_diameter_mm, internal_hu_mean, internal_hu_range, reference_parenchyma_hu, wall_thickness_mm, septations, calcification, soft_tissue_nodule).

task_completion.description: Assign a Bosniak category and provide a clinical recommendation based on the findings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The `bosniak_report.json` file is visible and explicitly contains a valid `bosniak_category` (I, II, IIF, III, or IV) and a populated `recommendation` field.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used medical imaging software to view and measure the CT scan.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the GUI of a medical imaging application (like 3D Slicer) with the CT scan loaded and interacted with, rather than the agent solely using terminal commands or scripts to guess values.

integrity.description: Verify the measurements in the JSON report match the visual markups.
integrity.visual_evidence: The diameter and HU values written in the `bosniak_report.json` should logically correspond to the values visible on the measurement markups in the imaging software screenshots.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the task by creating the JSON files without examining the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be a clear visual progression from opening the CT scan, actively placing markups, to finally generating the JSON files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the AMOS 2022 (Abdominal Multi-Organ Segmentation) dataset. The valid Bosniak categories for the classification are: I, II, IIF, III, and IV.
